description: Treating a successful pipeline run as proof of good data, so wrong or stale data flows downstream unchecked.
body: Failure mode: the only quality signal is whether the job exited zero, so a run that landed partial, stale, or malformed data is reported as healthy and the bad data propagates into every consumer. Detection signals: no data-level checks exist, only job status; downstream reports look off while pipelines are green; incidents are found by users, not monitors. Do not equate job success with data correctness. Safer fallback: add explicit schema, freshness, completeness, and uniqueness checks as a promotion gate separate from job status, and quarantine failures.
